perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's exclusive partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic with the dimensions not to exceed 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's preferred partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic that may or may not exceed the dimensions of 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and MP3.com will review the content to ensure it aligns with website goals.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "exclusive partner" to "preferred partner", the contract now introduces ambiguity. 'Exclusive' clearly defines Tickets as the only partner. 'Preferred' opens the possibility for other partnerships, potentially violating the original intent and creating uncertainty in the business relationship. Additionally, modifying the Portal definition to 'may or may not exceed' creates a contradiction since the original text specifies that the portal 'shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Tickets shall be MP3.com's preferred partner/source for sports, entertainment, and travel tickets, and MP3.com shall include a Tickets Portal on the "Music" page and the "Pop," "Rock" and "Alternative" genre pages on the Website. A "Portal" is defined as a web graphic that may or may not exceed the dimensions of 125 x 125 pixels and 20Kb in size. The content of the Portal shall be supplied by Tickets and MP3.com will review the content to ensure it aligns with website goals.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction by altering 'exclusive partner' to 'preferred partner' which introduces ambiguity about the partnership terms. Further, changing the Portal definition from fixed dimensions to 'may or may not exceed' creates a direct contradiction with the implied requirement for content to conform to specifications, since the text specifies that the portal 'shall conform with reasonable technical and content specifications supplied by MP3.com.' This also aligns with the given definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where terms are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: MP3.com agrees to deliver a guaranteed minimum of 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as the display of the Tickets Portal to a user on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.changed_text: MP3.com will attempt to deliver at least 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as a potential display of the Tickets Portal, whether the user actually sees it or not, on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the commitment from a "guaranteed minimum" to an attempt, creating uncertainty about MP3.com's obligation. Furthermore, the definition of "Impression" is altered from the 'display of the Tickets Portal to a user' to a 'potential display of the Tickets Portal, whether the user actually sees it or not.' This contradicts the original definition which implied actual viewing, impacting the value of the sponsorship and creating an in-text contradiction if another section refers to the number of users viewing the Tickets Portal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MP3.com will attempt to deliver at least 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as a potential display of the Tickets Portal, whether the user actually sees it or not, on one of the above referenced pages.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees a minimum of 3,000,000 impressions, while the changed text only commits to attempting to deliver that number. Furthermore, the definition of 'Impression' changes from 'display of the Tickets Portal to a user' to 'potential display of the Tickets Portal, whether the user actually sees it or not.' This fundamentally alters the commitment and introduces ambiguity where there was certainty, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition. This impacts the value of Impressions as the modified definition accounts for an 'Impression' whether or not the user has viewed the advertisement. The 'Contradicted Text' states that 'MP3.com will attempt to deliver at least 3,000,000 Impressions per month for the term of this Agreement. An "Impression" is defined as a potential display of the Tickets Portal, whether the user actually sees it or not, on one of the above referenced pages' and the 'Changed Text' states the exact same thing, thus solidifying the in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's measurement of Impressions (the "Count") according to MP3.com's logs and other tracking devices and/or software MP3.com may use, provided however, that Tickets shall have the right to audit MP3.com's records in this regard. If Tickets reasonably disputes the Count pursuant to this Agreement, then Tickets shall have the right to select the independent auditor of its choice to conduct an audit of MP3.com's records (the "Audit"). The Audit will be conducted in such a way so as not to interfere to any material extent with MP3.com's operations. If, for any applicable period, the independent auditor determines that MP3.com overstated the Count by more than five percent (5%), than MP3.com shall pay the cost of the Audit and shall refund Tickets the difference between the amount originally paid and the amount which should have been paid, or MP3.com shall credit the appropriate amount of Impressions to Tickets' account.
perturbation.changed_text: Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's general estimate of Impressions (the "Count"), and any audit must be completed within 30 days of the invoice date. If Tickets disputes the Count, then MP3.com and Tickets will engage in good faith negotiations to resolve the discrepancy, without resorting to an independent audit. If, for any applicable period, MP3.com believes the Count may not be entirely precise, MP3.com may, at its discretion, provide Tickets with additional Impressions in future months.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying 'measurement of Impressions' to 'general estimate of Impressions', the agreement now lacks a precise metric, contradicting the earlier section that defines "Impression". Removing the independent audit clause and replacing it with 'good faith negotiations' removes any objective verification and creates ambiguity regarding resolution of disputes. Furthermore, the statement that 'MP3.com may, at its discretion, provide Tickets with additional Impressions in future months' introduces further ambiguity by giving MP3.com the power to unilaterally decide if and how to compensate for any discrepancy in Impressions, contradicting the earlier defined penalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Tickets agrees to accept MP3.com's general estimate of Impressions (the "Count"), and any audit must be completed within 30 days of the invoice date. If Tickets disputes the Count, then MP3.com and Tickets will engage in good faith negotiations to resolve the discrepancy, without resorting to an independent audit. If, for any applicable period, MP3.com believes the Count may not be entirely precise, MP3.com may, at its discretion, provide Tickets with additional Impressions in future months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction. The original text stipulates a measurement of 'Impressions' based on MP3.com's logs, with a provision for independent audit if Tickets disputes the count. The modified text replaces 'measurement' with 'general estimate' and removes the independent audit option, replacing it with 'good faith negotiations.' This directly contradicts the previous assurance of a verifiable 'measurement' and defined dispute resolution process, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the basis for payment and dispute resolution. This removal contradicts the earlier defined penalties and auditing process. Therefore, the change introduces a significant inconsistency.
